skill.name: bmad-orchestrator
skill.description: Orchestrates BMAD workflows for structured AI-driven development. Routes work across Analysis, Planning, Solutioning, and Implementation phases.
skill.summary: Orchestrates BMAD workflows for structured AI-driven development across Analysis, Planning, Solutioning, and Implementation phases.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate BMAD workflow orchestration tool. The static analysis flagged 361 potential issues, but most are false positives. The only real concern is the pipe-to-shell pattern in the install script, which is a common industry practice for developer tool installation (similar to Docker, etc.). External command references are documentation examples, not actual execution. Network calls go to the legitimate plannotator.ai service.

medium_findings.title: Remote Script Execution via Pipe to Shell
medium_findings.description: The install script uses 'curl | sh' pattern to install plannotator CLI. This pipes a remote script directly to shell execution. While common for developer tools, this is an elevated risk pattern.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl | sh pattern is a known security concern but is standard practice for developer tool installation. The endpoint (plannotator.ai) is a legitimate service. User must explicitly run the install script.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged Ruby backtick syntax and shell command examples in documentation files (SKILL.md, SETUP.md, REFERENCE.md). These are example commands shown to users, not actual code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are example commands in documentation showing how to use the skill, not actual code execution paths. Documentation examples are intentionally shown to users.

low_findings.title: Document Hashing Misidentified as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged 'hash' references as weak cryptographic algorithms. This is incorrect - the skill uses document hashes to detect unchanged files for workflow management, not for security purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 'hash' references are for comparing document content to skip unnecessary re-reviews. This is a simple string comparison for workflow state management, not cryptographic hashing.

content.value_statement: BMAD workflow orchestrator helps AI development agents manage structured development across Analysis, Planning, Solutioning, and Implementation phases with built-in quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Initialize BMAD workflow configuration in any project
Check current workflow phase and status at any time
Route work across Analysis, Planning, Solutioning, and Implementation phases
Integrate with plannotator for document review quality gates
Manage structured handoff between workflow phases
limitations: Requires plannotator CLI installation for full phase gate review functionality
Works best with Claude Code, Gemini CLI natively; Codex/OpenCode require orchestration bridge
Does not execute code or interact with repositories directly

use_cases.title: Initialize New BMAD Project
use_cases.description: Set up BMAD workflow configuration for a new project with proper phase structure and documentation folders.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams starting new projects with AI-assisted workflows

use_cases.title: Phase Progress Tracking
use_cases.description: Check current phase, completed phases, and recommended next steps for the project.
use_cases.target_user: Project managers and developers tracking AI-driven development progress

use_cases.title: Quality Gates for Phase Documents
use_cases.description: Submit PRD, architecture, and other phase documents to plannotator for review before advancing to next phase.
use_cases.target_user: Teams requiring document review and approval before phase transitions

prompt_templates.title: Initialize BMAD Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /workflow-init to set up BMAD workflow in my project. Configure it for a web application with medium complexity.

prompt_templates.title: Check Current Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /workflow-status to see where we are in the BMAD process and what the next recommended phase is.

prompt_templates.title: Submit Document for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run bash scripts/phase-gate-review.sh docs/prd-myapp.md "PRD Review: myapp" to submit the PRD for plannotator review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Completing a phase and requesting review

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me complete the BMAD workflow: first run /workflow-init, then guide me through each phase until we reach Implementation. Submit each phase document for review before advancing.

output_examples.output: BMAD workflow initialized. Configuration created at bmad/config.yaml. Recommended first phase: Analysis (create product brief).

Current Phase: Planning
Completed: Analysis (product-brief-2026-03-13.md)
Next: Create PRD document
Phase Gate: Requires plannotator review before advancing to Solutioning

best_practices: Initialize BMAD at project start to establish clear phase structure
Run /workflow-status before each session to understand current phase
Always submit phase documents to plannotator for review before advancing
anti_patterns: Skipping phase gates by not using plannotator review
Advancing phases without completing required documentation
Creating documents outside the configured output folder

faq.question: What is BMAD?
faq.answer: BMAD stands for Business Model, Architecture, Design. It is a structured AI-driven development methodology that breaks work into four phases: Analysis, Planning, Solutioning, and Implementation.

faq.question: Which AI platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: Gemini CLI and Claude Code have native support. OpenCode and Codex require orchestration through omx/ohmg-style bridges.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything extra?
faq.answer: For full functionality, install the plannotator CLI by running bash scripts/install.sh from the skill directory.

faq.question: How do phase gates work?
faq.answer: Before advancing from one phase to the next, submit your phase document to plannotator for review. If the document hash matches a previous review, the gate is skipped.

faq.question: Can I customize the workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, edit bmad/config.yaml to customize output folders, project type, language settings, and other configuration options.

faq.question: What happens if I skip phase reviews?
faq.answer: You can skip plannotator reviews, but this defeats the quality gate purpose. The workflow allows it but recommends review before advancing.
